product_direction: Add a visible save-state label to the note editor header.

stakeholder_effects.effect: They get clearer save-state feedback before leaving the editor.
They may receive fewer avoidable questions about whether notes were saved.

unresolved_assumptions: The confusion comes from unclear save-state feedback rather than a real save failure.
A header label is noticeable enough without changing the editor workflow.
strongest_signal: Support tickets and replay observations both show editors hesitating around save-state confidence.
riskiest_claim: One visible header label will reduce save-state confusion without implying a stronger persistence guarantee.
main_uncertainty: Whether the label is noticeable enough to change behavior without overstating save guarantees.
main_trade_off: Ship a small reversible clarity improvement now while staying inside existing editor patterns and avoiding deeper workflow or persistence changes.

user.strongest_signal: Editors ask whether edits are saved and pause before leaving the editor.
user.weakest_assumption: The visible label addresses the actual moment of uncertainty.
user.disconfirming_evidence: Editors still pause or ask the same question after the label ships.
user.stop_condition: A five-person comprehension check shows the label is misunderstood.

evidence.strongest_signal: Two independent internal sources point to the same save-state uncertainty.
evidence.weakest_assumption: The sampled sessions represent normal editor behavior.
evidence.missing_evidence: A measured event for hesitation before leaving the editor.
evidence.disconfirming_evidence: Instrumented sessions show no change in exit hesitation after the label.
evidence.stop_condition: No measurement hook can distinguish label impact from normal editor use.

business.strongest_signal: The change is low cost, reversible, and aligned with support reduction.
business.weakest_assumption: Reducing save-state confusion is worth editor header attention.
business.missing_evidence: Longer-term effect on support volume and editor trust.
business.disconfirming_evidence: The label adds visual noise without reducing support contact or hesitation.
business.stop_condition: The change requires persistence, permission, notification, or external delivery work.

next_smallest_action: Implement one save-state label in the editor header, keep current save behavior unchanged, and measure exit hesitation plus support-tag volume.

must_not_do: expand scope or execute external irreversible actions
must_ask: new stakeholder, data, risk, or scope appears

stop_conditions: The implementation requires persistence, permission, notification, or external delivery changes.
A new stakeholder group is affected by the label.
The label implies a stronger save guarantee than the product can support.
